calendar.matters:
ACCOUNTING PETITION RE: FEES, FINAL DISTRIBUTION
Off Calendar Aug-30-2016 Granted Per Written Order
MINI MINUTES: Appearances: Donald Elwell-McFarland, petitioner in pro per

The Court noted his concerns that the fee request would need adjustment to reflect the payment to the document preparer, and also the value of the car to be retained by the petitioner. The Court also raised the question of the propriety of Mr. Elwell-McFarland as guardian holding a lien of the property of his ward. The petitioner stated that the lien would be paid off when the house was sold. The Court inquired when the interests in the real property would be distributed. Mr. Elwell-McFarland stated that he wanted his sister to be able to stay in the house until a decision was made regarding which high school she would attend in two years, at which point the plan would be to sell the house and move to the vicinity of her high school. The Court clarified that he was not requesting information on when the house was to be sold, but when the interests in the property would be transferred. The petitioner stated that he planned to make the transfer as soon as the court issued an order authorizing the transfer. The Court noted that there was need for a bond in the guardianship proceeding once the interest in the real property had been distributed to the
guardianship estate. The petition was taken under submission. The Court directed that a status hearing re receipt of distribution and re bond be set on September 20, 2016, at 9:00 am in Department 204 in guardianship proceeding PGN-15-299577.

Judge: Peter J. Busch. Clerk: Craig Blackstone. Bailiff: Deputy Grimaldi. (Not reported)

PETITION FOR PROBATE OF WILL AND FOR LETTERS TESTAMENTARY
Off Calendar May-05-2015 Granted Per Written Order
MINI MINUTES: Appearances: Donald D. McFarland, in Propria Persona.

The Court stated that this matter raises questions, that there is a less that perfectly put together will, that the will nominates Donald R. McFarland which is not who you are and asked about 3649 Shone Avenue. Mr. McFarland stated that there is no Donald R. McFarland, that it was a misspelling, that his sisters name was misspelled as well, that the 3649 Shone Avenue address is a home that he previously owned in Oakland which he sold prior to moving to Marin, that his sister also lived at that address, that his father traveled back and forth to the Philippines and that he became aware of the will 2 years ago. The Court asked for clarification of the date the will was signed (206=2006) but Mr. McFarland was not aware of the date. Mr. McFarland stated that the only thing left to them is the house, that his siblings are in agreement to keeping the house until the 11 year old sister completes high school, that he has contacted attorneys but all want to include the house in their equations and that he cannot afford the expense. The Court stated that all siblings are in accord at this time, that it sometimes becomes hard and strongly urged Mr. McFarland to seek legal advice. The Court granted the petition for probate of will and for letters testamentary based on the information before the court (subject to review of the order).

Judge: Peter J. Busch. Clerk: Leslie Gomez. Bailiff: Deputy Allen. (Not Reported)
